subject: RE:
body:
At the beginning of 2007 we compiled lists of possible pool consultants, the
lead of which was the contractor that installed the pool at Yacht Haven:
Certified Pools out of Florida, whose contact person is Harry Kashner.
Kashner at the time was also consulting with Manhattan at Botany Bay before
they were eliminated as contractors there. This company consulted with us
and the designers on the features of the pool which was originally designed
for the office structure. 

 

We simultaneously identified Pool Works and Sea Scape here as possibilities.
Kashner was chosen, however, due to the Yacht Haven connection and a skill
set stateside which could be imported here for a very high end  application.
Fountains and other more elaborate structures were also in their resume
which made them even more appropriate. You will recall that your original
main house design for the location where the library is now going at the
time had a very large two story fountain-waterfall in it and you have had
fountains under consideration from time to time for the existing compound.

 

We ended up in Florida because pool  contracting here, generally, is not
done by one, exclusive source. Typically, with the advice of a consultant
the pool is cast by a competent concrete contractor and then aerated and
finished under the direction of a specialist. 

 

When the pool was removed from the office and the main house plans changed
we did not continue to be in contact with all these sources. Construction
has slowed down here considerably and players come and go all the time.
Accordingly, there is a need to fish the local market again in response to
your request and to get current, reliable information about local
contractors. We have continued to be in contact with Certified Pools, whose
owner has visited our offices here to solicit business. They will be on the
list as well.  We should be able to give you a thoughtful and constructive
response in two or three days. 

 

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 
Sent: Monday, January 07, 2008 9:19 AM
To: John Amerling
Cc: DKIESQ@aol.com
Subject: Re:

 

why only now are we beginning to find a pool contractor? 

----- Original Message ----
From: John Amerling <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Monday, January 7, 2008 8:04:08 AM
Subject: RE:

Vivien Gerena, the project manager at ACOE was out all last week. I am
calling her shortly to arrange to meet her as soon as possible tomorrow or
Wednesday. At the same time I am trying to coordinate this visit with a site
review of one of the two pre-casting operations I know of in PR, as you
requested.  I will go to ACOE no matter what happens on the pre-casting
front. 

 

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 
Sent: Monday, January 07, 2008 8:56 AM
To: John Amerling
Subject: Re:

 

did you go to san juan ?

----- Original Message ----
From: John Amerling <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Monday, January 7, 2008 7:46:13 AM
Subject: RE:

Dan has a couple of names as a starter. I will, however, organize and give
you a complete response asap. I did call back on Saturday and I am ready to
speak with you at your convenience. 

 

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 
Sent: Sunday, January 06, 2008 6:35 PM
To: John Amerling
Cc: DKIESQ@aol.com; Emad Hanna
Subject: Re:

 

I want four local pool contractors, and the photos of their work by friday  

----- Original Message ----
From: John Amerling <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Friday, January 4, 2008 10:05:34 AM
Subject: 

Would you let me know when we could have a moment for a follow up
conversation? Thanks. 

 

Lafayette Contractors, LLC

6100 Red Hook Quarters, Suite A2-7

St. Thomas, VI 00802

340 779-8054 (O)

340 779-8055 (FAX)

340 690-7194 (Cell)

At the beginning of 2007 we compiled lists of possible pool consultants, the lead of which was the contractor that installed the pool at Yacht Haven: Certified Pools out of Florida, whose contact person is Harry Kashner. Kashner at the time was also consulting with Manhattan at Botany Bay before they were eliminated as contractors there. This company consulted with us and the designers on the features of the pool which was originally designed for the office structure.

 

We simultaneously identified Pool Works and Sea Scape here as possibilities. Kashner was chosen, however, due to the Yacht Haven connection and a skill set stateside which could be imported here for a very high end  application. Fountains and other more elaborate structures were also in their resume which made them even more appropriate. You will recall that your original main house design for the location where the library is now going at the time had a very large two story fountain-waterfall in it and you have had fountains under consideration from time to time for the existing compound.

 

We ended up in Florida because pool  contracting here, generally, is not done by one, exclusive source. Typically, with the advice of a consultant the pool is cast by a competent concrete contractor and then aerated and finished under the direction of a specialist.

 

When the pool was removed from the office and the main house plans changed we did not continue to be in contact with all these sources. Construction has slowed down here considerably and players come and go all the time. Accordingly, there is a need to fish the local market again in response to your request and to get current, reliable information about local contractors. We have continued to be in contact with Certified Pools, whose owner has visited our offices here to solicit business. They will be on the list as well.  We should be able to give you a thoughtful and constructive response in two or three days.
